# sqlalchemy_aio

{{ PyPI Version }} {{ Documentation }} {{ Travis }} {{ Coverage }} {{ MIT License }}

`sqlalchemy_aio` adds asyncio and [Trio] support to SQLAlchemy core, derived
from [alchimia].

## Getting started

```python
import asyncio

from sqlalchemy_aio import ASYNCIO_STRATEGY

from sqlalchemy import (
    Column, Integer, MetaData, Table, Text, create_engine, select)
from sqlalchemy.schema import CreateTable, DropTable


async def main():
    engine = create_engine(
        # In-memory sqlite database cannot be accessed from different
        # threads, use file.
        'sqlite:///test.db', strategy=ASYNCIO_STRATEGY
    )

    metadata = MetaData()
    users = Table(
        'users', metadata,
        Column('id', Integer, primary_key=True),
        Column('name', Text),
    )

    # Create the table
    await engine.execute(CreateTable(users))

    conn = await engine.connect()

    # Insert some users
    await conn.execute(users.insert().values(name='Jeremy Goodwin'))
    await conn.execute(users.insert().values(name='Natalie Hurley'))
    await conn.execute(users.insert().values(name='Dan Rydell'))
    await conn.execute(users.insert().values(name='Casey McCall'))
    await conn.execute(users.insert().values(name='Dana Whitaker'))

    result = await conn.execute(users.select(users.c.name.startswith('D')))
    d_users = await result.fetchall()

    await conn.close()

    # Print out the users
    for user in d_users:
        print('Username: %s' % user[users.c.name])

    # Supports context async managers
    async with engine.connect() as conn:
        async with conn.begin() as trans:
            assert await conn.scalar(select([1])) == 1

    await engine.execute(DropTable(users))


if __name__ == '__main__':
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    loop.run_until_complete(main())
```

## Getting started with Trio

To use the above example with [Trio], just change the following:

```python
import trio
from sqlalchemy_aio import TRIO_STRATEGY

async def main():
    engine = create_engine('sqlite:///test.db', strategy=TRIO_STRATEGY)

    ...

trio.run(main)
```

## What is this?

It's *not* an `asyncio` implementation of SQLAlchemy or the drivers it uses.
`sqlalchemy_aio` lets you use SQLAlchemy by running operations in a separate
thread.

If you're already using [run_in_executor] to execute SQLAlchemy tasks,
`sqlalchemy_aio` will work well with similar performance. If performance is
critical, perhaps [asyncpg] can help.

## Documentation

[The documentation] has more information, including limitations of the API.

[alchimia]: https://github.com/alex/alchimia
[asyncpg]: https://github.com/MagicStack/asyncpg
[run_in_executor]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio-eventloop.html#asyncio.AbstractEventLoop.run_in_executor
[the documentation]: https://sqlalchemy-aio.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
[trio]: https://github.com/python-trio/trio

classes.name: sqlalchemy_aio.exc.AlreadyQuit
documentation:
Raised by {class}`~sqlalchemy_aio.base.ThreadWorker` if an attempt is
made to use it after its thread has quit.

classes.name: sqlalchemy_aio.exc.BlockingWarning
documentation:
Emitted when an {class}`~sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncEngine` or
{class}`~sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncConnection` is used in a blocking
fashion accidentally.

For example, it is emitted in this case:

```python
engine = create_engine(..., strategy=TRIO_STRATEGY)
Table(..., autoload_with=engine)
```

classes.name: sqlalchemy_aio.exc.SQLAlchemyAioDeprecationWarning
documentation: Emitted for deprecated functionality.
DeprecationWarning is ignored by default on Python < 3.7, so use UserWarning

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.asyncio.Request.set_finished
documentation:
Needed to be executed in the same thread as the loop.
Since Event() is not thread-safe.

classes.name: sqlalchemy_aio.asyncio.AsyncioEngine
documentation:
Mostly like {class}`sqlalchemy.engine.Engine` except some of the methods
are coroutines.

classes.name: sqlalchemy_aio.trio.TrioEngine
documentation:
Mostly like {class}`sqlalchemy.engine.Engine` except some of the methods
are coroutines.

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncEngine._run_in_thread
documentation:
Unlike the public-facing `run_in_thread` method, we want this one
to let us call SQLAlchemy methods like normal internally.

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncEngine.run_in_thread
documentation:
Run a synchronous function in the engine's worker thread.

Example:

: The following blocking function:

  ```python
  some_fn(engine.sync_engine)
  ```

  can be called like this instead:

  ```python
  await engine.run_in_thread(some_fn, engine.sync_engine)
  ```

Parameters:

: func: A synchronous function.
  args: Positional arguments to be passed to `func`. If you need to

  > pass keyword arguments, then use {func}`functools.partial`.

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncEngine.sync_engine
documentation: Public property of the underlying SQLAlchemy engine.

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncEngine.connect
documentation:
Like {meth}`Engine.connect <sqlalchemy.engine.Engine.connect>`, but
returns an awaitable that can also be used as an asynchronous context
manager.

Examples:
: ```python
  conn = await engine.connect()
  await conn.execute(...)
  await conn.close()
  ```

  ```python
  async with engine.connect() as conn:
      await conn.execute(...)
  ```

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncEngine.begin
documentation:
Like {meth}`Engine.begin <sqlalchemy.engine.Engine.begin>`, but
returns an asynchronous context manager.

Example:
: ```python
  async with engine.begin():
      await engine.execute(...)
  ```

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncEngine.execute
documentation:
Like {meth}`Engine.execute <sqlalchemy.engine.Engine.execute>`, but
is a coroutine that returns an {class}`AsyncioResultProxy`.

Example:
: ```python
  result = await engine.execute(...)
  data = await result.fetchall()
  ```

:::{warning}
Make sure to explicitly call {meth}`AsyncioResultProxy.close` if the
{class}`~sqlalchemy.engine.ResultProxy` has pending rows remaining
otherwise it will be closed during garbage collection. With SQLite,
this will raise an exception since the DBAPI connection was created
in a different thread.
:::

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncEngine.scalar
documentation:
Like {meth}`Connection.scalar <sqlalchemy.engine.Engine.scalar>`,
but is a coroutine.

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncEngine.has_table
documentation:
Like {meth}`Engine.has_table <sqlalchemy.engine.Engine.has_table>`,
but is a coroutine.

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncEngine.table_names
documentation:
Like {meth}`Engine.table_names <sqlalchemy.engine.Engine.table_names>`,
but is a coroutine.

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncEngine.run_callable
documentation:
Like {meth}`Engine.run_callable        <sqlalchemy.engine.Engine.run_callable>`.

:::{warning}
This method blocks. It exists so that we can warn the user if
they try to use an async engine for table reflection:

```python
Table(..., autoload_with=engine)
```
:::

classes.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncConnection

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncConnection.run_in_thread
documentation:
Run a synchronous function in the connection's worker thread.

Example:

: The following blocking function:

  ```python
  some_fn(conn.sync_connection)
  ```

  can be called like this instead:

  ```python
  await engine.run_in_thread(some_fn, conn.sync_connection)
  ```

Parameters:

: func: A synchronous function.
  args: Positional arguments to be passed to `func`. If you need to

  > pass keyword arguments, then use {func}`functools.partial`.

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncConnection.sync_connection
documentation: Public property of the underlying SQLAlchemy connection.

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncConnection.execute
documentation:
Like {meth}`Connection.execute <sqlalchemy.engine.Connection.execute>`,
but is a coroutine that returns an {class}`AsyncioResultProxy`.

Example:
: ```python
  result = await conn.execute(...)
  data = await result.fetchall()
  ```

:::{warning}
Make sure to explicitly call {meth}`AsyncioResultProxy.close` if the
{class}`~sqlalchemy.engine.ResultProxy` has pending rows remaining
otherwise it will be closed during garbage collection. With SQLite,
this will raise an exception since the DBAPI connection was created
in a different thread.
:::

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncConnection.connect
documentation:
Like {meth}`Connection.connect <sqlalchemy.engine.Connection.connect>`,
but is a coroutine.

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncConnection.scalar
documentation:
Like {meth}`Connection.scalar <sqlalchemy.engine.Connection.scalar>`,
but is a coroutine.

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncConnection.close
documentation:
Like {meth}`Connection.close <sqlalchemy.engine.Connection.close>`,
but is a coroutine.

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncConnection.closed
documentation: Like the {attr}`Connection.closed        <sqlalchemy.engine.Connection.closed>` attribute.

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncConnection.begin
documentation:
Like {meth}`Connection.begin <sqlalchemy.engine.Connection.begin>`,
but returns an awaitable that can also be used as an asynchronous
context manager.

Examples:
: ```python
  async with conn.begin() as trans:
      await conn.execute(...)
      await conn.execute(...)
  ```

  ```python
  trans = await conn.begin():
  await conn.execute(...)
  await conn.execute(...)
  await trans.commit()
  ```

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncConnection.begin_nested
documentation:
Like {meth}`Connection.begin_nested        <sqlalchemy.engine.Connection.begin_nested>`, but returns an awaitable
that can also be used as an asynchronous context manager.

:::{seealso}
{meth}`begin` for examples.
:::

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncConnection.in_transaction
documentation: Like {meth}`Connection.in_transaction        <sqlalchemy.engine.Connection.in_transaction>`.

methods.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base.AsyncConnection.run_callable
documentation:
Like {meth}`Connection.run_callable        <sqlalchemy.engine.Connection.run_callable>`.

:::{warning}
This method blocks. It exists so that we can warn the user if
they try to use an async connection for table reflection:

```python
Table(..., autoload_with=connection)
```
:::

Mostly like {class}`sqlalchemy.engine.Connection` except some of the
methods are coroutines.

classes.name: sqlalchemy_aio.base._BaseContextManager
documentation: Allow `async with <coroutine>` or `await <coroutine>`.
